zones.main_reason: High temperature, critically low green cover, groundwater depletion

recommended_species.name: Khejri
recommended_species.reason: Best suited for dry Rajasthan climate and sandy soil

recommended_species.name: Neem
recommended_species.reason: Heat tolerant and improves air quality

recommended_species.name: Banyan
recommended_species.reason: Provides high shade and long-term ecological benefit

implementation_plan: Start plantation before monsoon
Use native drought-resistant species
Create water harvesting pits near plantation zones
Monitor survival rate every 30 days

zones.main_reason: Urban heat island effect, critically low green cover, severe AQI problem

recommended_species.name: Peepal
recommended_species.reason: 24-hour oxygen producer, ideal for urban heat reduction

recommended_species.name: Neem
recommended_species.reason: Excellent air purifier and heat tolerant

recommended_species.name: Arjun
recommended_species.reason: Suited for Delhi NCR climate and improves air quality

implementation_plan: Prioritise roadside and park plantation in Phase 1
Use root barrier systems near underground utilities
Select pollution-tolerant species for roadsides
Coordinate with municipal corporations for urban plantation drives

Focus on agroforestry model for farmer income supplementation
Plant Teak and Shisham on degraded forest land
Use rainfall season for zero-cost plantation drives

zones.main_reason: Flood buffer plantation priority, riverbank erosion control

recommended_species.name: Arjun
recommended_species.reason: Deep roots stabilise riverbanks and control erosion

recommended_species.name: Bamboo
recommended_species.reason: Rapid growth creates flood buffer and soil binder

recommended_species.name: Jamun
recommended_species.reason: Thrives in high-rainfall alluvial zones

implementation_plan: Focus plantation on Ganga and Sone riverbanks first
Use Bamboo belts as primary flood buffers
Plant Arjun trees along embankment slopes
Involve local panchayats in plantation maintenance

zones.main_reason: Critically low green cover, extreme heat, saline soil degradation

recommended_species.name: Khejri
recommended_species.reason: Exceptionally drought and saline soil tolerant

recommended_species.name: Neem
recommended_species.reason: Salt-tolerant, grows in Kutch conditions

recommended_species.name: Bamboo
recommended_species.reason: Stabilises sandy soil and provides ground cover quickly

implementation_plan: Use drip-irrigated nurseries for initial seedling phase
Select only halophyte-compatible species
Coordinate with Gujarat Forest Dept for Miyawaki dense plantation
Create wind-break plantation rows to stop soil erosion
